title: Kubernetes Security Advisories - 2022-11-08
description:
Merged 2 kubernetes security advisories published on 2022-11-08. Original advisory IDs: CVE-2022-3162, CVE-2022-3294

Titles:
- Unauthorized read of Custom Resources
- Node address isn't always verified when proxying
affected: kubernetes:### How do I mitigate this vulnerability?
kubernetes:Upgrading the kube-apiserver to a fixed version mitigates this vulnerability.
kubernetes:Prior to upgrading, this vulnerability can be mitigated by avoiding granting cluster-wide list and watch permissions.
kubernetes:Requests containing `..` in the request path are a likely indicator of exploitation. Request paths may be captured in API audit logs, or in kube-apiserver HTTP logs.
kubernetes:If you find evidence that this vulnerability has been exploited, please contact security@kubernetes.io
kubernetes:This vulnerability was reported by Richard Turnbull of NCC Group as part of the Kubernetes Audit.
kubernetes:Upgrading the **kube-apiserver** to a fixed version mitigates this vulnerability.

kubernetes:Aside from upgrading, configuring an [egress proxy for egress to the cluster network](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/extend-kubernetes/setup-konnectivity/) can mitigate this vulnerability.

kubernetes:Fix impact:** In some cases, the fix can break clients that depend on the nodes/proxy subresource, specifically if a kubelet advertises a localhost or link-local address to the Kubernetes control plane.

kubernetes:Node create & update requests may be included in the Kubernetes audit log, and can be used to identify requests for IP addresses that should not be permitted. Node proxy requests may also be included in audit logs.
kubernetes:This vulnerability was reported by Yuval Avrahami of Palo Alto Networks.

affected_products.product: This vulnerability was reported by Yuval Avrahami of Palo Alto Networks.

fixed_versions: Requests containing `..` in the request path are a likely indicator of exploitation. Request paths may be captured in API audit logs, or in kube-apiserver HTTP logs.
If you find evidence that this vulnerability has been exploited, please contact security@kubernetes.io
This vulnerability was reported by Richard Turnbull of NCC Group as part of the Kubernetes Audit.

Fix impact:** In some cases, the fix can break clients that depend on the nodes/proxy subresource, specifically if a kubelet advertises a localhost or link-local address to the Kubernetes control plane.

Node create & update requests may be included in the Kubernetes audit log, and can be used to identify requests for IP addresses that should not be permitted. Node proxy requests may also be included in audit logs.
This vulnerability was reported by Yuval Avrahami of Palo Alto Networks.
